page: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal NVMe family. It is powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 4245P processor operating at a base frequency of 3.8 GHz, configured with 6 physical cores and 12 threads. The system features 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.67 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no hardware GPU accelerators present on this instance.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression tests ranking in the top 10% of all tested servers. Passmark memory performance also places in the top tier. Conversely, multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, align with average tier expectations. A primary hardware tradeoff is the large-block memory write bandwidth, which registers in the bottom 25% at 23.57 GB/sec, whereas read bandwidth remains in the average tier.

This bare metal server provides dedicated hardware resources, eliminating virtualization overhead and noisy-neighbor issues. The high single-core clock speed and top-tier single-threaded performance make it highly efficient for workloads sensitive to single-core latency. The 1920 GB NVMe SSD offers substantial local storage capacity, though the 32.0 GB memory limit restricts its suitability for massive in-memory databases. It is best suited for static web serving, single-threaded data processing, small-model LLM text generation, and moderate database hosting.
description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring an AMD EPYC 4245P processor with 6 physical cores, 12 vCPUs, and a 3.8 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD, with no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and memory scores, though multi-core metrics and database operations fall in the average range. A notable tradeoff is the large-block memory write bandwidth, which ranks in the bottom 25%. Given its dedicated hardware, high single-core speed, and substantial local NVMe storage, this server is suited for latency-sensitive single-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a bare metal server with a 3.8 GHz AMD EPYC 4245P CPU, 12 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for dedicated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-6c-32gb-amd is a bare metal server featuring a 12-vCPU AMD EPYC processor, 32 GB RAM, and 1920 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: Dedicated bare metal compute featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing and fast local NVMe storage for latency-sensitive workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core execution and database operation speeds

Suited for static web serving, small LLM inference, and dedicated database hosting
